findings.topic: Chatting commoditization & margin compression
findings.title: Chatting is now commoditized — compete on front-end marketing, not back-end chat
findings.claim: Chatting has been commoditized to revshare deals as low as 5%, so agencies can no longer differentiate on chat quality and must compete on organic social marketing instead.
findings.what_they_do: This consultant advises agencies to deprioritize chatting as a differentiator and instead invest heavily in organic social content production as the primary value proposition to creators — arguing that front-end traffic generation is now the only defensible competitive moat.
findings.vs_us: Palm already outsources chatting (§8) and has its strongest system in organic social content production (§1), which aligns directly with this thesis. However, Palm has not explicitly framed its organic social strength as the core competitive differentiator when pitching/signing creators (§6 GAP).
findings.recommendation: Incorporate this framing explicitly into creator acquisition pitches: lead with Palm's data-driven content engine (§1) as the primary value-add, not chat. When building the in-house chat roadmap (§8), treat it as table-stakes infrastructure, not a differentiator. Document this positioning in the acquisition SOP.

sources.quote: Chatting has now become completely commoditized. Today, just about every agency understands the core principles of good chat and has a fundamentally identical offer
in 2026 and beyond, the greatest value you can possibly offer a new model is on the front end

findings.topic: AI chatbot displacement of human chatters
findings.title: AI chatbots will soon displace low-cost human chatters — build AI chat now
findings.claim: AI chatbots are improving rapidly and will soon deliver low-latency, high-quality chat at a fraction of the cost of even the cheapest human VAs, making human chatting teams economically unviable.
findings.what_they_do: The consultant flags AI chat as an imminent market force that will undercut human chatters (including cheap Filipino VA teams) on both cost and latency, framing it as a structural shift rather than a distant future trend.
findings.vs_us: Palm's roadmap already includes an AI chatting project (§8), which directly anticipates this shift. This finding is high-confidence validation that Palm's AI chat roadmap is correctly timed and should be accelerated rather than deprioritized.
findings.recommendation: Use this as justification to prioritize the AI chatting roadmap project. When evaluating build vs. buy, note that the window to build a proprietary advantage is narrowing as third-party AI chat vendors improve. Capture competitor AI chat implementations (scripts, fan-tier logic, PPV sequencing) as build-toward intelligence per §8.

sources.quote: AI chat bots are making huge improvements very quickly and will soon become a dominant force in the market, delivering low latency, high quality chat at a fraction of the cost of even the cheapest of Filipino virtual assistants

findings.topic: Paid and low-quality traffic channels declining ROI
findings.title: Reddit ads, paid ads, and SFS swaps are margin-killers — abandon them for organic
findings.claim: Agencies still spending on Reddit, paid ads, SFS, and GG swaps are watching already-thin margins deteriorate and should reallocate entirely to organic social.
findings.what_they_do: The consultant explicitly names Reddit spend, paid advertising, SFS (shoutout-for-shoutout), and GG (girl-girl) swaps as legacy tactics that are now net-negative when factoring in declining ROI and margin compression, advocating a full pivot to organic social content.
findings.vs_us: Palm's traffic/distribution strategy is not documented (§5 GAP). It is unknown whether Palm currently uses Reddit spend, SFS, or paid ads. This is a direct signal to audit any current spend on these channels and document a clear traffic strategy position.
findings.recommendation: Audit whether any Palm creators or the agency itself currently spends on Reddit ads, paid ads, or SFS arrangements. If so, measure ROI rigorously. Document Palm's official traffic strategy (currently a §5 GAP). Given Palm's mature organic content engine (§1), codify organic-first as the explicit traffic doctrine and deprioritize paid/SFS unless ROI is proven.

sources.quote: Many agencies are still using the old methods, throwing money at Reddit and paid ads and SFS and GG swaps, and their already razor thin margins are getting worse by the day

findings.topic: Organic social as primary agency scaling lever
findings.title: Scale organic social ops as the #1 agency growth lever in 2026
findings.claim: The largest OFM agencies earning hundreds of millions annually built their scale on excellent organic social content years ago, and the rest of the industry must catch up now.
findings.what_they_do: The consultant frames organic social content production — not chatting infrastructure or paid acquisition — as the defining competency separating top-tier agencies from the rest, calling for agencies to laser-focus on producing excellent creative and scaling their social media operations.
findings.vs_us: Palm already has a mature, data-driven organic social pipeline (§1) built around viral inspo scraping and an 8-step production workflow. Palm is ahead of most competitors here. However, Palm's real-creator posting cadence (frequency, time-of-day) is not codified (§1 GAP), which limits the ability to truly 'scale' social ops systematically.
findings.recommendation: Palm should codify the real-creator posting cadence (frequency, platform-specific timing) to complete the §1 system. Additionally, document Palm's organic social capability as a formal competitive differentiator for use in creator acquisition pitches (§6). Consider producing case-study evidence of social growth results to use in signing conversations.

sources.quote: 2026 is the year you have to get good at organic social. It is time to laser focus on producing excellent creative and scaling your social media ops
the platforms merely ask for one thing in return. Just stop making dog shit. Create content that people actually want to watch

findings.topic: Creator brand building as a distinct discipline from niche selection
findings.title: Build distinct creator brands (audience + identity + story) — not just niches
findings.claim: Most OFM agencies confuse 'brand' with 'niche,' but a real creator brand answers three questions: who is your audience, what is the creator's unique identity, and what story/fantasy are you selling.
findings.what_they_do: The consultant uses a three-question brand framework — (1) specific audience demographic beyond 'men who like porn', (2) unique creator identity that inspires devotion, (3) specific fantasy/story and emotional levers to pull — and claims this framework has taken creators from zero to top 0.1% earning hundreds of thousands per month. He cites Camila and Bonnie Blue as examples of brand-driven success.
findings.vs_us: Palm has no documented creator brand framework (§2 GAP and §1 partial). The content pipeline (§1) is strong on viral ideation but does not appear to systematically define creator identity, target audience persona, or narrative/fantasy positioning before deciding what content to film. This is a meaningful strategic gap in how Palm directs creators.
findings.recommendation: Adopt a structured brand-definition step upstream of the content pipeline (§1). Before a creator enters the inspo/filming cycle, document: (1) their specific target audience segment, (2) their unique identity differentiators vs. comparable creators, (3) the core fantasy/emotional narrative they sell. Store this in the Creators table in Airtable and use it as a filter when promoting inspo reels to creators in Step 5 of the pipeline.

sources.quote: most of OFM doesn't know what brand actually means or confuses the concept of brand with niche
brand and only fans boils down to three questions. The first question is who is your audience? The second question is what is your identity? The third and final question is what is your story?

findings.topic: Agency value proposition positioning for creator signing
findings.title: Lead creator pitches with organic social capability, not chat or back-end ops
findings.claim: The value proposition that closes creator deals in 2026 is demonstrable front-end marketing capability — organic social reach and traffic generation — not chatting or business infrastructure.
findings.what_they_do: The consultant advises that agencies pitching creators should lead with their organic social marketing system and track record of growing audiences, since that is what creators cannot do themselves and cannot easily get elsewhere at differentiated quality.
findings.vs_us: Palm's acquisition system is Josh-DM-led with no systematic pitch framework documented (§6 GAP). It is unknown whether current pitches lead with Palm's organic social strength. Given Palm's mature content engine (§1), this capability should be front-and-center in every creator conversation but is not yet formalized.
findings.recommendation: Build a formal creator pitch deck or one-pager that leads with Palm's organic social system: data-driven inspo sourcing, AI-assisted analysis, and proven posting infrastructure. Include any growth metrics from existing creators. This directly addresses the §6 GAP in systematic acquisition and gives Josh a repeatable, differentiating pitch rather than ad-hoc DM conversations.

sources.quote: the greatest value you can possibly offer a new model is on the front end. Using organic social to create marketing campaigns and distribution networks that generate massive volumes of high quality traffic

findings.topic: Content categorization & usage-tracking system
findings.title: Label every asset with platform-usage codes to avoid reuse conflicts
findings.claim: Tagging each image file with short codes (e.g. RS, RN, T, PPV) for every platform it has appeared on prevents accidental reuse and lets chatters/managers instantly see which assets are still available for a given platform.
findings.what_they_do: Each uploaded image is numbered sequentially and given a suffix string of platform codes: RS = Reddit selfie subreddit, RN = Reddit niche/kink subreddit, T = Tinder/Bumble, PPV = uploaded to OF vault. A legend document defines all codes. When selecting content for a new post the manager scans the suffix — an image showing only 'T' has never been used on Reddit and is free to post there. Once used, the new code is appended. Content is pre-sorted into category folders: Kinks, Lingerie, Nude/NSFW, Reddit-specific, Social Media (SFW).
findings.vs_us: Palm's content pipeline (§1, §2) tracks Assets and Tasks in Airtable and manages editing/approval/scheduling, but there is no documented system for tracking which individual asset has been deployed to which external platform (IG, Reddit, dating apps, OF feed vs vault). The distinction between OF vault/PPV assets and social assets exists structurally but cross-platform deduplication is not codified (§2 is THIN).
findings.recommendation: Add a 'Platform Usage' multi-select or tag field to the Airtable Assets table (or a linked 'Deployments' table) that logs each platform an asset has been posted to. This prevents duplicate posts across Reddit, dating apps, IG, and OF, and gives chatters a clear view of what PPV content is available and unused. A simple legend doc should accompany the field definitions.

sources.quote: you number them all one two whatever and then there's a letter for each instance it's been used on that platform
rsrnt which means it's been used on the Reddit selfie, the Reddit Niche and dating apps which pretty much means you can't really use that image again

findings.topic: Dating-app image fingerprinting awareness
findings.title: Dating apps fingerprint images at upload — new photos required every cycle
findings.claim: Tinder (and similar apps) use Amazon Rekognition to create a text-based fingerprint of every uploaded photo, so metadata removal and even pixel-level cloaking software will not prevent re-used images from getting the account shadow-banned or banned.
findings.what_they_do: The agency maintains strict separation of images used on dating apps from all other platforms. Once an image has been uploaded to Tinder/Bumble it is permanently marked (T code) and never reused there. Fresh, previously undeployed photos are sourced for each new dating-app account cycle. The tracking system described above makes this operationally feasible.
findings.vs_us: Palm does not have a documented dating-app traffic strategy (§5 GAP, §6 GAP). If/when Palm or managed creators use Tinder/Bumble as a fan-acquisition channel, this fingerprinting constraint must be built into the asset-tracking system from day one to avoid bans.
findings.recommendation: When Palm builds out a dating-app traffic strategy, bake the 'never reuse a T-flagged image' rule into the Asset tagging system before launch. Brief any VA or chatter who touches dating-app accounts on the Rekognition fingerprinting issue so they do not attempt metadata-stripping workarounds.

sources.quote: if you try to reuse images whether you have removed the metadata or not on dating apps the app Flags the account and it will either get Shadow ban or band
they lease Amazon recognition software which ultimately lists every photo uploaded to the database as a text-based fingerprint

findings.topic: Google Drive backup & folder replication
findings.title: Replicate local folder structure 1-to-1 on Google Drive as business-continuity backup
findings.claim: If the agency's local machine were destroyed, the business should still be able to operate — so every folder and file that is critical to operations is mirrored on Google Drive in the exact same structure.
findings.what_they_do: The agency maintains a Google Drive that mirrors the local computer: a top-level 'Raw' folder per creator containing sub-folders for raw content, photo grid, invoices, promotional assets, OF stories, and the services agreement (contract). The Raw folder is shared directly with the model (right-click → share access → pink highlight convention so the manager knows it is externally shared) so models can self-upload content.
findings.vs_us: Palm uses Airtable + Cloudflare media for asset storage (§1) and Dropbox for invoice delivery (§7). A formal business-continuity / disaster-recovery policy for media assets is not documented. The model self-upload workflow exists (creators upload to become Assets) but the specific shared-folder colour-coding convention and the 'can the business function without this laptop' checklist mindset are not codified.
findings.recommendation: Document a formal backup policy: define which data stores are business-critical (Airtable, Cloudflare media, contract PDFs, invoices) and ensure at least one redundant copy exists outside the primary system. For creator onboarding, consider a shared Google Drive or Cloudflare folder (colour/tag-coded as 'creator-accessible') as the upload landing zone, mirroring the model-upload convenience described here.

sources.quote: if my laptop dropped or fell in a pool or caught on fire or lightning hit it or a car ran it over could the business still operate if the answer is no then I back everything up to the Google Drive
the Royal folder pink that means that this folder has been shared with the model

findings.topic: OF vault folder structure for chatter efficiency
findings.title: Organise OF vault into named script folders so chatters can locate PPV assets instantly
findings.claim: Structuring the OF content vault into named folders (e.g. 'Script 1', 'New Subscriber', 'PPV', 'SFW Feed') tied to specific chat scripts reduces chatter errors and speeds up PPV delivery during live conversations.
findings.what_they_do: Inside the OF vault the agency creates dedicated folders for: (1) each reusable sexting script (Script 1, Script 2, etc.) containing all media needed for that session; (2) a 'New Subscriber' folder with content specifically used in the new-sub welcome sequence; (3) a general 'PPV' folder; (4) optionally an 'SFW' folder for feed reposts. When a chatter replays a script with a new subscriber they navigate to the matching script folder to pull the correct assets in order.
findings.vs_us: Palm's OF vault/PPV content system is THIN (§2) and chatting is currently outsourced (§8). There is no documented vault folder convention or script-to-asset mapping. This is directly relevant to Palm's roadmap item of building an in-house chat team and AI chatting system.
findings.recommendation: As build-toward for the in-house chat team: design the OF vault structure around scripts rather than raw chronology. Create a folder schema (New Subscriber, Script_01…Script_N, PPV_unlabelled, SFW_Feed) before handing off to chatters. Document which assets belong to which script in Airtable so the AI chatting system can programmatically select the right media during a conversation.

sources.quote: if you have big sexting scripts...you'll create folders in the vault called like script one script two Etc which is where all the content relating to that script will go
the easier you make it for them the less chance of mistakes there will be

findings.topic: Content category taxonomy for OF vs social
findings.title: Use a five-category taxonomy to separate OF feed, PPV, SFW social, dating-app, and Reddit-specific content
findings.claim: Explicitly separating content into distinct category buckets (Kinks, Lingerie, Nude/NSFW, Reddit-specific, Social Media SFW) prevents giving away premium content for free on the OF feed and ensures the right content reaches the right platform.
findings.what_they_do: Five named folders per creator: (1) Kinks — fetish-specific; (2) Lingerie — semi-explicit, suitable for OF feed teaser (avoids giving away too much); (3) Nude NSFW — for sale only, not posted free; (4) Reddit — platform-specific content including niche/kink subreddit variants (e.g. pixelated content for specific nerd subreddits); (5) Social Media SFW — safe-for-work used on IG, Twitter, dating apps. The agency explicitly states it does not recommend giving away nude content for free.
findings.vs_us: Palm's pipeline (§1) categorises content through the 8-step inspo flow and separates social from OFTV (§2), but there is no documented five-bucket content taxonomy distinguishing OF feed teasers from PPV vault content from Reddit-specific variants from SFW social. The social vs OF vault split is structural but the sub-categories within each are not defined.
findings.recommendation: Adopt or adapt a formal content taxonomy in Airtable's Assets table. At minimum add a 'Content Category' single-select field with values: SFW-Social, Lingerie-Feed, NSFW-PPV, Reddit-Specific, Kink-Fetish. This enables filtering, chatter-facing views, and ensures editors and chatters never accidentally schedule premium content to the free feed.

sources.quote: you've Got The Kinks then you got lingerie which is applicable for any not safe for work kind of setting like only fans particularly the only fans feed because you don't want to give away too much
Reddit gets its own folder because subreddits require verifications you'll have to create content that is specific to subreddits on Reddit

findings.topic: Invoicing & paper trail
findings.title: Generate a formal invoice every time a model payment is made to maintain a legal paper trail
findings.claim: Sending a professional invoice with every model payment creates a paper trail that protects the agency legally and signals operational legitimacy to creators.
findings.what_they_do: Every creator payment triggers an invoice document stored in its own 'Invoices' sub-folder inside the creator's Google Drive directory. The services agreement (contract) is also stored in the same creator folder for easy retrieval.
findings.vs_us: Palm already generates weekly invoices automatically via Puppeteer PDF + Dropbox + Resend email (§7), so the core behaviour is matched. Palm also stores contracts at onboarding (§6). The incremental difference is that this agency co-locates invoices and contracts inside the creator's shared Google Drive folder, making them accessible to the creator directly — Palm currently delivers invoices by email rather than a shared folder.
findings.recommendation: Minor enhancement only: consider adding a 'Documents' section to the creator's shared folder (or a creator portal page) where they can view all past invoices and their contract on demand, reducing support requests and reinforcing professionalism.

sources.quote: inside will be categorized like this...you'll also have a photo grid invoices because each time we send a payment to the model we create an invoice remember you've got to be professional you've got to leave a paper truck

findings.topic: Reddit account farming & karma seasoning
findings.title: Build a perpetual stockpile of karma-farmed Reddit accounts via VA team
findings.claim: Running Reddit at scale requires a dedicated VA farming team that continuously creates and seasons accounts to a minimum karma threshold so you always have ready inventory to rotate in when accounts get banned.
findings.what_they_do: Agency runs a team of VAs (plus automation) that create Reddit accounts daily, warm them for 2-3 days acting as normal users, then farm post karma (target: 2,000) and comment karma (target: 300) by posting in meme/advice subreddits. Accounts are stockpiled; when a posting account gets banned a seasoned replacement is pulled immediately from the pile. VAs are paid per account that hits the karma threshold. Automation now supplements VAs for scale.
findings.vs_us: Palm has no documented Reddit strategy at all (§5/§2 GAP — traffic/distribution not documented). This is pure build-from-zero territory. Palm's Apify scraping and Publer infra exists for IG/TikTok but nothing maps to Reddit account farming or karma seasoning.
findings.recommendation: If Palm adds Reddit to its traffic stack, do NOT start with black-hat scale. Begin with the expert's own minimum viable advice: one dedicated VA, one phone, one account per creator, organic posting to well-matched subreddits daily. Only invest in a farming pipeline after proving per-creator ROI. Document karma thresholds (2,000 post / 300 comment) as acceptance criteria for any farmed account.

sources.quote: we have an account creation system every day. there's new accounts being created and there's just a farming team
An account is done seasoning if it hits 2,000 post karma and 300 common karma

findings.topic: Reddit subreddit targeting strategy
findings.title: Scrape a 100k-subreddit master list keyed to creator physical attributes, then niche-match posts
findings.claim: Rather than manually searching for relevant subreddits, build a scraped database of 100,000+ subreddits tagged by niche attributes so any new creator can be immediately mapped to every viable subreddit based on her physical characteristics.
findings.what_they_do: Agency built (or uses) a scraper that pulls every subreddit and tags it by niche (ethnicity, body type, fetish, etc.). When a creator is onboarded, her attributes (blonde, big breasts, e-girl, etc.) are matched against the database to generate her posting list. Volume over precision: one piece of content gets posted across every marginally relevant subreddit. Key insight: NSFW content also gets cross-posted into SFW subreddits (makeup, car repair, GeoGuessr) using curiosity/cleavage tactics to reach users who have never enabled NSFW settings.
findings.vs_us: Palm has no Reddit presence (§5 GAP). Palm's content pipeline (§1) does use Apify scraping for IG inspiration, so the scraping capability exists — it would need to be redirected to Reddit subreddit discovery. The SFW infiltration angle is completely absent from any Palm workflow.
findings.recommendation: Commission a one-time scrape of Reddit subreddits tagged by adult-content niche to build a master lookup table. Store in Airtable alongside creator attribute fields. On creator onboarding, auto-generate a subreddit posting list. Prioritise NSFW subreddits first; layer in SFW curiosity subreddits (lifestyle, hobby) only once the core strategy is proven.

sources.quote: what we would do is we would just have a scraper that would basically scrape every single subreddit that would or could be tied to either one of these niches. You'd build up a list of subreddits. You'd have a 100,000 subreddits

there's also a lot of say for work subreddits where people would just, you know, I don't know, ask for advice for piercings in their ears or or makeup advice. And you could also post in these subreddits

findings.topic: Reddit content strategy vs. Instagram content strategy
findings.title: Use high-volume 7/10 static images on Reddit — never repurpose IG hook-driven reels
findings.claim: Reddit rewards posting volume of decent static/short-clip content across many subreddits, not the single viral hook-driven reel optimised for Instagram; ten 7/10 images outperform two 10/10 videos on Reddit.
findings.what_they_do: Agency repurposes a bank of ~100 photos per creator to sustain a full year of Reddit posting without new shoots. Videos used on Reddit are simple (10-15 sec, body-focused, no hook/narrative) and the same clip is reposted across 100 subreddits simultaneously. No time-of-day optimisation — just maximum post frequency spread across subreddits. Goal is aggregate impressions across subreddits, not a single viral post.
findings.vs_us: Palm's content system (§1) is engineered entirely around IG hook-driven reels — viral scoring, transcript analysis, inspo-driven filming. That pipeline produces the wrong content type for Reddit. Palm also has thin OF vault content (§2); Reddit uses a middle-ground asset type (SFW-ish or soft NSFW stills/clips) that neither pipeline currently covers.
findings.recommendation: Define a third content tier for Reddit: simple, high-quality still photos and 10-15 sec body-focused clips. These can be captured as a small add-on during existing shoots. Store in Airtable as a separate asset type ('Reddit Assets'). Do not route IG reels to Reddit — they are wrong-format and waste the hook work.

sources.quote: give me a hundred pictures like like have a creator give me 100 pictures and I can promote her for the rest of the year with those 100 pictures
I would much rather have 10 pieces of content that are like seven out of 10, then I would have three pieces of content, two or three pieces of content that are 10 out of 10

findings.topic: Reddit-to-OF funnel mechanics
findings.title: Use Reddit profile bio as the single OF conversion point; layer IG or Twitter as intermediate funnel where direct OF links are banned
findings.claim: 99% of NSFW subreddits prohibit OF links in posts but allow them in the Reddit profile bio, making profile visits the core conversion mechanic; where even bio links are banned, route traffic through Twitter or Instagram as an intermediate step.
findings.what_they_do: Standard setup: OF link in Reddit profile bio only (not in post body or comments). Post content drives profile clicks; profile bio converts to OF. Where subreddits ban OF bio links (e.g. after saturation): place IG or Twitter link in bio instead, grow those accounts as a secondary funnel, then convert to OF from there. This also unlocks access to additional subreddits that block OF links but permit social links. Reddit DMs used sparingly as a tertiary funnel (limited by Reddit ban risk on high-volume DM sending).
findings.vs_us: Palm has no Reddit funnel documented (§5 GAP). Palm does run IG accounts and has Publer scheduling (§5), so the IG intermediate-funnel variant aligns with existing infrastructure. The Twitter/X layer is absent from Palm's current stack.
findings.recommendation: When testing Reddit, default to OF link in bio as the primary CTA. Before launching a creator on Reddit, audit which target subreddits permit OF bio links vs. only social links, and set up the appropriate intermediate account (IG preferred, as Palm already operates IG). Track Reddit → IG → OF conversion separately from direct Reddit → OF to measure intermediate-funnel efficiency.

sources.quote: basically 99% of the subreddits allows you to post it on your profile on your Reddit profile in the bio. That's it.
instead of getting all these fans and getting to getting them to the only fans straight away… you could also say, okay, I want to funnel them to, for example, an Instagram account

findings.topic: Reddit LTV vs. other traffic sources
findings.title: Reddit organic NSFW traffic produces the highest fan LTV of any channel — $8–$35 on free pages
findings.claim: Fans acquired through niche-matched NSFW Reddit posts have significantly higher LTV than Instagram or dating-app traffic because they self-selected into a subreddit specifically for their fetish and actively chose to view the content, indicating high purchase intent.
findings.what_they_do: Agency benchmarks LTV by traffic source. Reddit organic NSFW: $8–$35 LTV on free pages. Dating app traffic also historically high LTV. Instagram traffic lower. Agency prioritises Reddit despite declining ROI trend because subscriber quality (intentionality) remains superior. At peak (3 years ago) one viral SFW post could drive 3,000–5,000 subs/day; currently realistic target is 10+ paid subs/day per account on a well-matched creator.
findings.vs_us: Palm has no documented traffic-source LTV benchmarking (§9 GAP — no written retention/churn strategy). Palm's whale-hunting dashboard (§9) tracks per-fan spend but does not appear to attribute fans to acquisition channel. This LTV signal is highly actionable for Palm's channel prioritisation decisions.
findings.recommendation: Add acquisition-channel tagging to Palm's Fan Tracker CRM (§9) so LTV can be broken down by source (IG reel, Reddit, etc.). Even before launching Reddit, build the measurement infra so that if/when Reddit is tested, LTV comparison data is captured from day one. Use the $8–$35 Reddit LTV benchmark as the hurdle rate when evaluating Reddit's cost-per-acquisition against account farming costs.

sources.quote: we do see a way way higher LTV from these fans off of Reddit, off of the regular Reddit strategy than we see from Instagram fans
I think it's safe to say that that's the best way to get the highest um highest quality of subscriber to the account

findings.topic: Reddit minimum viable setup for new agencies
findings.title: Start Reddit with one VA, one phone, one account per creator — no scheduling tools, no black hat
findings.claim: Any agency new to Reddit should start with the simplest possible setup — a dedicated VA with a phone-based account posting organically to well-matched subreddits — before investing in black-hat infrastructure, because even this baseline can generate 10+ paid subs/day.
findings.what_they_do: Expert's explicit minimum recommendation regardless of agency size: assign one VA per creator for Reddit, have them use a physical phone (not desktop — Reddit deprioritises desktop activity), post organically every day or every other day to keep the account warm, no scheduling software, no automation, no upvote manipulation. Find the right subreddits through manual trial first. This single-account approach can yield 10+ paid subs/day on a well-matched creator with hundreds of viable organic NSFW subreddits available.
findings.vs_us: Palm currently uses Publer for scheduling (§5) and Telegram routing to Amin for manual posting. The phone-native, no-scheduler Reddit requirement is architecturally different. Palm has no VA role defined for Reddit posting (§10 GAP — org structure thin). This is the lowest-risk entry point for Palm.
findings.recommendation: If Palm pilots Reddit for even one creator, assign a VA (or redirect Amin) to phone-based manual posting. Do not route Reddit through Publer — Reddit flags scheduler signatures. Allocate 30 days of organic posting to 20–50 well-matched NSFW subreddits before evaluating whether black-hat investment is warranted. Track subs attributed to Reddit via UTM or unique landing page.

sources.quote: get a dedicated VA. Not no scheduling, no post scheduling, nothing. If you're new to Reddit, get a dedicated VA… just posts every day in or every other day to keep the account warm
one account could definitely get you, let's say, anywhere upwards from 10 paid subs per day

findings.topic: Reddit automation infrastructure for scale
findings.title: Reddit at scale (500+ posts/day) requires a full-time in-house developer — it cannot be outsourced or off-the-shelf
findings.claim: Posting at scale on Reddit (hundreds of posts per day across many accounts) is impossible to do manually and requires custom-built automation maintained by a full-time developer who is embedded in the team, because Reddit continuously changes its systems and breaks off-the-shelf tools.
findings.what_they_do: Agency employs a full-time developer whose primary job is building and maintaining Reddit automation: account creation pipelines, karma farming bots, posting schedulers, and proxy/anti-detect management. When Reddit changes its platform, the developer patches the systems. Currently runs ~2 full-time humans monitoring and managing captions/targeting on top of the automation layer. Reduced headcount from ~20+ contractors to ~6 fixed contractors by automating the posting layer.
findings.vs_us: Palm has Evan as tech/product (§10) and an existing Apify + Next.js + Airtable stack (§0). Palm could theoretically build Reddit automation on top of existing infra, but it would represent a significant new development investment. Palm has no Reddit automation currently (§5 GAP).
findings.recommendation: Do not attempt Reddit at scale without dedicated developer capacity. Palm should treat Reddit automation as a distinct engineering project, not a VA task. If/when Reddit is added to Palm's traffic stack and proves ROI at the single-account level, scope a Reddit automation module as a separate build-plan item. Use the 500-posts/day benchmark as the threshold at which manual VA posting becomes unscalable and automation investment is justified.

sources.quote: posting 20 posts from 100 accounts every day. That's 2,000 posts a day. That's not something a VA can do… you need to automate things like that
you are basically forced to hire a developer… He needs to be part of the team

findings.topic: Hiring process rigor
findings.title: Multi-stage hiring funnel with hard filters before any offer
findings.claim: A slow, multi-stage hiring funnel with strict filtering criteria is cheaper than hiring the wrong person, whose true cost in lost time, training, and mistakes runs into tens of thousands of dollars.
findings.what_they_do: Agency runs a dedicated hiring funnel lasting roughly one week: job post → multiple filtering stages → skills/personality tests → pick only the single best-qualified applicant. If no applicant clears the bar, the owner does the job themselves for another 2 weeks and re-runs the funnel rather than settling. Once hired, the employee gets a fully thought-through onboarding, individual KPI tracking, and formal performance reviews.
findings.vs_us: Palm's hiring is not formally documented (§10 — THIN). There is no written hiring funnel, scorecard, or onboarding SOP. Josh currently drives acquisition and ops largely himself, which matches the 'doing it yourself while you find the right person' fallback, but the structured multi-stage filter does not exist.
findings.recommendation: Design a formal 2–3 stage hiring funnel for each role Palm needs next (e.g., editor, chatter, VA): job post → async task/test → live screen → offer. Define a written 'must-have' scorecard per role before opening any search. Document the cost-of-bad-hire math internally so the team doesn't rush to fill seats.

sources.quote: set up a direct hiring funnel that may even take like a week to get all the applicants

findings.topic: Daily employee feedback loop
findings.title: Daily feedback cadence to accelerate employee ramp 7x vs weekly
findings.claim: Giving employees feedback every single day instead of once a week makes them progress seven times faster, cutting ramp time from seven months to thirty days.
findings.what_they_do: Agency owner or a designated lead delivers individual feedback to every employee daily — not a team standup, but specific corrective or reinforcing input tied to that person's KPIs and tasks. This is paired with formal performance reviews on a recurring schedule.
findings.vs_us: Palm has no documented feedback cadence for editors, Amin, or any contractor (§10 — THIN). There are no performance review cycles or KPI dashboards per employee in the codebase. The closest analogue is the editor task queue in the portal, which surfaces output but not a feedback loop.
findings.recommendation: Establish a lightweight daily async check-in for each active role (editor, posting contractor, eventually chatters): a shared Telegram thread or Airtable comment thread where the reviewer flags one specific 'fix this' and one 'good job' per day per person. Formalise this before hiring the in-house chat team (~1 yr roadmap item, §8).

sources.quote: give your employees feedback once a week, I give them feedback every single day. That means my employees are going to progress seven times faster

findings.topic: KPI tracking and A/B testing culture
findings.title: Track all agency KPIs and A/B test every variable before committing to it
findings.claim: Knowing your exact PPV purchase rate, chatting ratio, average reply time, Instagram conversion, views-per-sub, and LTV — and A/B testing every script or tactic in 3+ variants — is the operational discipline that separates scaling agencies from stuck ones.
findings.what_they_do: Agency maintains a live spreadsheet where the owner can quote every core metric from memory or within 2 minutes. For any new script or tactic, they run 3 simultaneous variants across chatters, track unlock/purchase rates per variant, identify the winning element (e.g., a different opening angle), and roll that element into all other scripts. Testing is weekly and continuous.
findings.vs_us: Palm has partial analytics infrastructure: the Whale Hunting dashboard and Fan Tracker CRM (§9 — PARTIAL) capture fan-side data, and the content pipeline tracks viral scores (§1 — MATURE). However, there is no documented agency-level KPI dashboard covering PPV purchase rate, chatter reply time, or IG conversion (§7 — GAP, §9 — GAP). A/B testing of chatting scripts is not possible today because chatting is outsourced (§8 — OUTSOURCED).
findings.recommendation: Build a single 'Agency Health' Airtable view or admin dashboard page tracking at minimum: IG follower growth rate, IG-to-OF conversion %, OF subscriber count, PPV purchase rate, and revenue per creator. As the in-house chat team is built (§8 roadmap), wire A/B test tracking into chatter SOP from day one — log script variant, message count, and revenue outcome per conversation.

sources.quote: what's your PPV purchase rate, what's your chatting ratio, your average reply time, your Instagram conversion, how many views do you need to make per sub

findings.topic: A/B testing chatting scripts
findings.title: Run 3-variant script tests with unlock-rate tracking before standardising any chatting SOP
findings.claim: Running three concurrent script variants with measured unlock rates — rather than just deploying a new script agency-wide — reveals the specific element driving performance and prevents agencies from unknowingly using inferior scripts.
findings.what_they_do: When a new chatting script is introduced, the agency assigns variant A, B, and C to different chatters or conversation batches, tracks PPV unlock rate per variant, diagnoses why the winning variant beat the others (e.g., opening line framing), and only then canonises the best approach across all chatters.
findings.vs_us: Chatting is currently outsourced at Palm (§8 — OUTSOURCED); no internal script management or test infrastructure exists today. This is a build-toward finding for the planned in-house and AI chatting projects.
findings.recommendation: When designing Palm's in-house chat SOP (§8 roadmap), build variant testing into the process architecture from day one: store script variants in Airtable with a linked 'test result' table capturing unlock rate and revenue per test run. Do not import a competitor's script wholesale — always test 3 variants first.

sources.quote: test every single one of them. Okay, this script got a 50% unlock rate. The other variation is 30%, this is 55. Okay, why?

findings.topic: Diversification against single-point-of-failure bans
findings.title: Treat every revenue-generating asset as likely to disappear tomorrow; diversify across creators, accounts, and traffic sources
findings.claim: Because Instagram accounts, OF accounts, and individual creators can all disappear overnight without warning, any agency with revenue concentrated in a single asset is one ban or personal decision away from zero.
findings.what_they_do: Agency explicitly flags concentration risk as a daily operational concern: monitors the revenue share of each creator, each Instagram account, and each employee. If one creator generates the majority of revenue, the agency treats that as a red flag and accelerates recruiting to dilute the dependency. Same logic applied to Instagram accounts (build more traffic sources, have a ban-response protocol) and key employees (have them document and train backups before they leave).
findings.vs_us: Palm's AI persona infrastructure already provides meaningful diversification (multiple accounts, hardware-isolated, §3/§4 — MATURE). However, on the real-creator side, creator concentration risk is not formally tracked (§9 — PARTIAL; §7 — GAP). There is no documented ban-response protocol or creator-departure contingency plan in the codebase.
findings.recommendation: Add a 'revenue concentration' metric to the agency health dashboard: flag any single creator, IG account, or traffic channel that exceeds 40% of total revenue. For the top-concentration creator, document a written contingency plan (which backup account takes traffic, who covers DMs, how fast can recruiting replace volume). For IG accounts, formalise the ban-response protocol currently referenced but not codified.

sources.quote: You have one creator that makes the majority of the revenue, that should be a fucking red flag. That should scare the hell out of you.

findings.topic: Creator recruiting as a permanent, dedicated department
findings.title: Treat creator recruiting as a full internal department that never stops, not an ad-hoc activity
findings.claim: Recruiting is the primary scaling lever for an OFM agency because creator quality and quantity directly determine revenue ceiling, yet most agencies treat it as an afterthought rather than a dedicated, always-on department.
findings.what_they_do: Agency runs recruiting as a named department with dedicated effort and headcount, not delegated to the CEO's spare time. The principle is that the ideal creator (great look, great work ethic, fully cooperative) effectively makes the agency's systems and marketing effort secondary — and that five such creators could produce $200–300k/month. Recruiting never pauses regardless of current creator count.
findings.vs_us: Palm's acquisition is currently Josh-DM-led with no systematic machine (§6 — THIN). The 75-conversation market research shows intentionality, but there is no documented recruiting SOP, dedicated headcount, or outbound funnel for creators. This is the agency's most documented GAP.
findings.recommendation: Prioritise building a creator acquisition system as a named workstream: define an ideal creator profile (look, niche, work ethic, platform readiness), document an outbound DM sequence and qualification rubric, and assign or hire someone to run it full-time as volume scales. Track pipeline metrics (outreach sent, response rate, signed rate) in Airtable. Do not pause recruiting when the current roster feels full.

sources.quote: Your recruiting shouldn't stop. Your recruiting shouldn't be left on the side. It's a full department in your agency that if you don't take it seriously, you're always going to be stuck.

findings.topic: Creator quality as a marketing force multiplier
findings.title: A high-quality creator reduces the marketing effort required; a weaker creator demands more sophisticated content to compensate
findings.claim: The less compelling a creator is intrinsically (look, on-camera presence, work ethic), the more the agency must compensate with superior content production and marketing — meaning creator quality and content effort are inversely proportional.
findings.what_they_do: Agency uses this as a calibration principle when evaluating both new creator prospects and current portfolio: a top-tier creator posting a basic dance reel may outperform a mediocre creator's elaborate multi-part production. The agency explicitly looks for creators who are unique (not just conventionally attractive) and reliable, treating those attributes as revenue multipliers on top of any content system.
findings.vs_us: Palm's content system is data-driven and productised (§1 — MATURE), which is the correct compensation mechanism for creators who need more marketing lift. However, there is no documented creator quality scoring rubric at acquisition (§6 — THIN/GAP) that would let Palm predict how much content effort a given creator will require. The two systems should be linked.
findings.recommendation: At creator onboarding, score each prospect on a simple rubric: uniqueness/look, on-camera presence, work ethic/responsiveness, and niche fit. Use that score to set internal expectations for content investment level (high-quality creator = standard pipeline; lower-quality creator = additional content formats, stronger hooks, more volume). Feed this back into acquisition criteria so Palm prioritises signing creators who need less marketing lift.

sources.quote: The less interesting your creator gets, the more you have to make up for it with better marketing.
